prompt:
A Venue Booking & Operations Management System that includes booking milestone communications, capacity-controlled inventory, member entitlements, asset lifecycle governance, evaluation workflows, payments/refunds, and group communication for on-site programs. The backend exposes resource-oriented APIs for Authentication (local username/password only), Users/Members, Bookings, Inventory & Fulfillment, Notifications, Groups, Assets, Evaluations, and Payments. Roles are Administrator (global configuration and audits), Operations Manager (inventory, fulfillment timing, message templates), Finance (payments, refunds, reconciliation), Asset Manager (asset master data and versioning), Evaluator/Reviewer (evaluation actions within assigned scope), and Member (bookings, preferences, points). Messaging is implemented as an in-app notification center with configurable channels limited to offline-capable delivery; templates support variables (e.g., booking_id, start_time, pickup_point) with strict type validation and a preview render API, and triggers fire at confirmation, T-24 hours upcoming, schedule change, cancellation, and exception states. Do Not Disturb defaults to 9:00 PM-7:00 AM local time and suppresses non-critical reminders; user preferences allow opt-out per category, with delivery receipts recorded per attempt and graceful fallback limited to internal inbox when other channels are unavailable. Inventory supports both real-time decrement and pre-deduction at booking creation; oversell protection is enforced by atomic reservations with a 15-minute hold timeout, after which holds auto-release. Sellable inventory and fulfillment cutoffs are configurable by pickup point or delivery zone; items auto-unpublish when available_qty reaches 0 and generate internal restock alerts to Operations. Membership supports tiers (e.g., Silver/Gold/Platinum) based on rolling 12-month spend, points accrual at 1 point per $1.00 net (excluding tax and refunds), redemption in increments of 100 points, optional stored-value wallets with manual top-ups only (offline), and blacklist/abnormal behavior rules that can freeze redemption for 30 days with audit reason codes.

Data is persisted in PostgreSQL and accessed via Diesel within an Actix-web API service running as a single Docker-deployable node. Core tables include users (unique username, salted password hash, role enum, status), members (tier, points_balance, wallet_balance, blacklist_flag), bookings (state, start/end timestamps, inventory_hold_expires_at, change_reason), inventory_items (sku, pickup_point_id/zone_id, available_qty, safety_stock, publish_status) with an inventory_ledger (immutable append-only deltas, source_type, correlation_id unique for idempotency), notifications (template_id, channel enum, payload_hash, delivery_state, read_at, dnd_suppressed), groups and group_messages (read receipts per user), assets (asset_code unique, classification, brand/model, procurement_date, cost, useful_life_months, depreciation_method enum, owner_unit, responsible_user_id, status) plus asset_versions for historical traceability, and evaluations (cycle_id, participant_scope, due_at, state machine with allowed transitions and node-level permissions). Payments/refunds are constrained to offline-capable recording and settlement: the system stores payment intents, timeout auto-close at 30 minutes, supports partial refunds with cumulative refund cap <= original paid amount, reconciliation imports via local file upload with checksum validation, and exception compensation as manual adjustments requiring Finance approval and immutable audit logs. Security requires at-rest encryption for sensitive fields (e.g., wallet balances, procurement cost) or masking in responses by role, plus tamper-evident audit trails for tier changes, points adjustments, refunds, and asset edits. Non-functional targets include p95 API latency < 300 ms for standard queries on a single node, optimistic concurrency on mutable aggregates, daily local backups, structured logs with correlation IDs, and deterministic job scheduling for reminders and hold expirations without any external network dependencies.
